retry_reason: The operation never fails; it is invoked when the kref reaches zero and will always free the object. Retry is meaningless.
contract_summary: This is the release/deallocation function for c4iw_ep, invoked by kref_put() when the reference count reaches zero. It conditionally releases a QP reference, removes a TID entry, frees the dst entry, releases an L2T entry, frees an MPA skb, purges any pending skb queue, and releases the wr_wait reference, followed by freeing the ep structure itself. The caller (kref_put) must ensure that no further usage of the ep occurs after this function is called, as the object is freed.

judge.reason: This function is a kref release callback; its sole purpose is to destroy the c4iw_ep object and release all associated resources (QP, TID, dst, l2t, skb, wr_wait) when the last reference is dropped.

check.reason: No refcount operation on the parameter 'kref' is present in the function code. The function is a kref release callback and does not explicitly decrement the kref's refcount. The candidate operation is not supported.

The candidate operation (put on parameter 1) is not present in the code. The function's primary purpose is cleanup of internal resources, not reference-count management. The judge's classification as a wrapper was incorrect.
